aliases: Pork broth rice noodle meal set

english: Pork broth rice noodle meal set

region_of_origin: Vietnam / Singapore (Vietnamese cuisine served in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Noodle soup meal bundle with starter and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Hu Tieu or Vietnamese rice noodle soup meal bundle. FOODON_00002473 (noodle soup food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR (Soups and broths) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Hu Tieu or Vietnamese rice noodle soup in FoodEx2 v2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Hu Tieu or Vietnamese rice noodle soup meal bundle.

serving.description: 1 meal bundle (1 bowl Hu Tieu noodle soup + 1 starter + 1 beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal bundle serving consists of approximately 650 g total, comprising one bowl of Hu Tieu noodle soup (approximately 450 g), one starter portion such as spring rolls or salad (approximately 100 g), and one beverage (approximately 100 g liquid component counted).

nutrient_source.title: Pho Bo (Vietnamese beef noodle soup) and Vietnamese rice noodle soup — adapted composite estimate using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for rice noodle soup components and HPB Singapore Foodo analogous Vietnamese noodle dish data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Hu Tieu meal bundle exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from: (1) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Soup, noodle, rice, Vietnamese style' (FDC ID 172949) providing per-100g baseline for the noodle soup component; (2) HPB Singapore Foodo analogous Vietnamese noodle soup dish data for regional calibration; (3) composite adjustment for the meal bundle format including starter and beverage. The per-100g values represent the blended meal bundle as consumed. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — rice noodles and analogous Vietnamese noodle soup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hu Tieu meal bundle. GI of 53 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for rice noodles (GI approximately 53, low category) as the primary carbohydrate vehicle. This is consistent with published GI values for rice vermicelli and Vietnamese-style rice noodle soups in Atkinson et al. 2021. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 59.8) / 100 ≈ 31.7, rounded to 31.8. Serving carbohydrate estimated at 9.2 g/100g × 650 g serving = 59.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from pork bone broth and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the broth, as the pork bone soup base contributes an estimated 420 mg sodium per 100 g, with a full meal bundle potentially delivering 2,700 mg or more sodium, substantially exceeding the recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from rice vermicelli noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that while the GI of rice noodles is low to moderate (GI ~53), the full meal bundle delivers approximately 60 g of carbohydrate per serving, resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 32, which requires careful portion management and should be paired with adequate protein and fibre to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork and sodium from broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of this meal bundle due to its combined saturated fat contribution from pork-based ingredients and high sodium load from the broth, both of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes; requesting reduced-sodium broth and limiting pork fat portions is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hu Tieu (Hủ Tiếu) is a Vietnamese rice noodle soup dish with origins in southern Vietnam and strong Cambodian-Chinese culinary influences, widely served in Vietnamese restaurants across Singapore. The Hu Tieu Meal Bundle as offered at Viet'Spice (Circular Road, Singapore) consists of one bowl of Hu Tieu noodle soup, one starter (typically Vietnamese spring rolls or a fresh salad), and one beverage. The soup features a clear, deeply flavoured pork bone broth simmered for several hours, served over thin, smooth rice vermicelli noodles (bún or hủ tiếu noodles), and topped with sliced pork, shrimp, fish cake, bean sprouts, spring onions, fried shallots, and fresh herbs. The broth is characteristically lighter and sweeter than phở, with a clean umami depth derived from pork bones, dried shrimp, and daikon. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (~72 kcal/100g), provides a meaningful protein contribution from pork and seafood, and has a low-to-moderate glycemic index (~53) due to the rice noodle base. However, the sodium content is high due to the broth, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The meal bundle format at SGD 38.90 represents a complete single-person dining experience. Clinically, portion control of the noodle component and broth consumption is recommended for diabetic and hypertensive individuals.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Hu Tieu?
Is Hu Tieu high in sodium?
What is the difference between Hu Tieu and Pho?
What is in a Hu Tieu meal bundle?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Hu Tieu meal bundle exists in any approved primary source (HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, USDA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous Vietnamese rice noodle soup entries and HPB Singapore Foodo regional calibration. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle values. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation rather than direct measurement.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172949, Vietnamese-style rice noodle soup) and HPB Singapore Foodo analogous data; no exact approved-source entry for Hu Tieu meal bundle exists. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food type. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food type. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food type. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy analogous entry for Vietnamese noodle soup; no added hydrogenated fats in traditional preparation). GI set to 53 based on analogous rice noodle GI from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021). Glycemic load per serving calculated at 31.8 based on 650 g serving size and 9.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Serving size of 650 g is an estimate for the full meal bundle (soup bowl + starter + beverage liquid component). Halal status is unconfirmed for this specific restaurant; pork is a primary ingredient so is_halal is set to false. The meal bundle price of SGD 38.90 is preserved from the field survey source record for reference only and is not used as a nutritional evidence source.

Verified ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Verified multilingual coverage for Singaporean cuisine (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) plus Vietnamese origin name.
